title: CCT capability registry
description: Tool-neutral capability model (FR-029). catalog.yaml defines capabilities once; each <adapter>.yaml classifies them in two dimensions: implementation kind x runtime status. No adapter may invent a capability id that the catalog does not define.

schema_version.description: Incremented when the record shape changes incompatibly.

kind.description: 'catalog' defines capabilities; 'adapter' classifies them for one tool.

adapter.description: Adapter id (required when kind = adapter), e.g. 'pi', 'claude-code'.

implementation_kind.description: How the capability is provided: by the host tool itself, authored by CCT, an optional bridge, or an external platform CCT does not implement.

runtime_status.description: Observed state. 'unavailable' and 'unsupported' are properties of the platform; 'disabled' and 'misconfigured' are properties of this installation.

default.description: Status assumed before any probe runs.

requires.description: Capability ids that must be enabled for this one to function.

conflicts.description: Capability ids that cannot be enabled alongside this one.

claude_equivalent.description: Corresponding Claude Code surface, or null when there is none. Never claim parity that does not exist.

status_probe.description: How runtime_status is determined for this adapter.

docs.description: Repo-relative path or URL documenting the capability.

reason.description: Required whenever runtime_status is not 'enabled' — the honest explanation surfaced by features/doctor.
